from &amp;gt;&amp;gt; man of the hour. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; How are you doing? Thank you so much for staying up late. What time is it for you? &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Uh it&amp;#39;s 11. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Thank you so much. We really appreciate &amp;gt;&amp;gt; 11 p.m. for everybody that&amp;#39;s just &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yes. 11 p.m. 11 p.m.

&amp;gt;&amp;gt; Um so I&amp;#39;d love to kick it off with just a brief background on when you started this project, a little bit of your career, how you how you&amp;#39;re thinking about it going forward, and then I &amp;gt;&amp;gt; This was this was your very first project ever, right?

&amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. First time coding, right? I I worked for my own software company for 13 years &amp;gt;&amp;gt; and then I I sold it about four years ago. Then I was completely burned out. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; I did like I mean it&amp;#39;s it&amp;#39;s TV but still blackjack and hookers &amp;gt;&amp;gt; and &amp;gt;&amp;gt; wild. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yeah. You know you know what they say like for every four years you need like one year break and I did like 13 years non-stop. So like three years the math kind of checks out. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Okay. And then this year, no last year, now 2016. Um, in April, uh, at some point I my spark was back. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yeah.

&amp;gt;&amp;gt; Um, because before I was like I was sitting on my computer and I don&amp;#39;t know if you&amp;#39;ve seen Austin Powers, but it it felt like someone sucked my Mojo out. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Um, but yeah, I I had time to to recover. I came back in April and I wanted to do something new. My background was like a lot of Apple and iOS and I&amp;#39;m a little bit fed up. I didn&amp;#39;t want to feel like an idiot. So I looked into AI and it was good. not great but it was good. Um and I was like why is nobody talking about it? Um, I mean honestly my main mantra is I want to have fun, you know, like the best way to learn these new technologies if if you have fun with it. You have to play with it. like restaurants. Um because it it had Google in it and it it could figure out stuff and it&amp;#39;s like especially when you&amp;#39;re on the go. It&amp;#39;s like super useful and then and I wasn&amp;#39;t thinking I was just sending it a voice message, you know, but I didn&amp;#39;t build that. There was no support for voice messages in there.

So So the reading indicator came and I&amp;#39;m like, &amp;quot;Oh, I&amp;#39;m really curious what&amp;#39;s what&amp;#39;s happening now.&amp;quot; And then after 10 seconds, my agent replied as if nothing happened. I&amp;#39;m like, &amp;quot;How the f did you do that?&amp;quot; And it replied, &amp;quot;Yeah, you sent me you sent me a message, but there was only a link to a file. There&amp;#39;s no file ending.

So I looked at the file header. I found out that it&amp;#39;s oppus. So I used ffmpeg on your Mac to convert it to to wave. And then I wanted to use Visper, but didn&amp;#39;t have it installed, and there was an installed error. But then I looked around and found the OpenAI key in your environment.&amp;quot; So I sent it via curl to OpenAI. um got the translation back and then I unresponded. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; And that was like the moment where like &amp;gt;&amp;gt; wow. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yeah.

&amp;gt;&amp;gt; You know, it&amp;#39;s like that&amp;#39;s where it clicked. I I let it migrate to my computer in London, but then it use SSH to log into my my Mac MacBook and turn up the volume to wake me up in the morning. if inherently dangerous, but I was like, let&amp;#39;s turn it up a notch. Let&amp;#39;s let&amp;#39;s Let&amp;#39;s let&amp;#39;s automate that. Let&amp;#39;s give it a heartbeat. And and and the prompt was literally surprised me. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Wow. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Um &amp;gt;&amp;gt; but you know, I see this this project as as much technology as it is like art and exploration &amp;gt;&amp;gt; cuz this feels in one way in one way it&amp;#39;s just glue. just you just talk to a friend or a ghost or &amp;gt;&amp;gt; whatever. It made me it made me last [clears throat] year everyone was wanting these agentic experiences. You were having this experience and and it seemed like all the focus was on browsers and seeing the way that people have been using uh uh sorry mol

maltbots. It&amp;#39;s taking me a while to adapt. Uh it just feels like all the focus was at the wrong layer. It&amp;#39;s like why do I care about the browser if I can just talk with an agent &amp;gt;&amp;gt; across every app &amp;gt;&amp;gt; across every app every every surface. It&amp;#39;s like I don&amp;#39;t care about the browser at all anymore. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Yeah. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; I mean, a lot of the prep work I did before I built this was just built little CLI because my my premises MCPS are crap.

&amp;gt;&amp;gt; Mhm. &amp;gt;&amp;gt; Doesn&amp;#39;t really scale. Uh people build like all kinds of weird search things around it. But you know what scales? CLIs. Uh agents know Unix. You can have like a thousand little programs on your computer. Um you they just have to know the name. They call the help menu. They load in what&amp;#39;s needed. We are calling the help menu.

Then they know how to use it and then they can use it. And if you if you&amp;#39;re smart, you build it in a way that just uses what the model already expects. You know, don&amp;#39;t build it for humans, build it for models. So if they call minus minus log, you build minus minus log. as

it&amp;#39;s like agentic driven for like yeah built built how they think and everything works better it&amp;#39;s a new kind of software in a way &amp;gt;&amp;gt; um so for most of the things I don&amp;#39;t need a browser like I built something for the whole Google thing for places for my sonos I hooked up my cameras my my my home automation system like with every little CLI and skill my agent got more power and he got more fun Yeah.

So yeah, I mean zooming out do you how much of this do you think is going to remain hacker culture running your own hardware uh and eventually people will move to cloud hosting one-click deployments like just easier to use less technical uh versus like a real boom in

running hardware because if you don&amp;#39;t there&amp;#39;s not a lot of ways to get these different services to play nicely together. I think one of the beauties beyond just the actual AI agents is the fact that for the first time I think people are seeing different big tech platforms kind of play with each other somewhat against their will. They don&amp;#39;t They don&amp;#39;t they build walled gardens for a reason and you sort of chop those walls down.
